title: 10 best herbal teas for puffy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for puffy eyes are infusions made from various plants that can help reduce the appearance of puffy eyes. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe the skin around the eyes and decrease swelling. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for puffy eyes include chamomile, green tea, and calendula. To use these teas, simply steep a tea bag in hot water, let it cool, and then place it on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Not only can this help reduce puffiness, but it can also improve the overall appearance of the eye area, making individuals feel more confident and refreshed. Many people have reported that incorporating herbal teas into their skincare routine has helped them achieve a more youthful and well-rested appearance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with puffy eyes because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and soothe the skin around the eyes. The caffeine in white tea can also help to constrict blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, white tea is a natural source of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to tighten and firm the skin. Regularly drinking white tea or using it as a compress can help to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes and promote healthy, refreshed-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness in eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing swelling, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in white tea helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, reducing the appearance of puffiness, thanks to its rich catechin and polyphenol content.

Anti-aging: White tea's anti-aging properties help maintain skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, which can contribute to the appearance of puffy eyes, due to its abundance of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechins.
Diuretic: White tea has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention, which can cause puffiness in the eyes, due to its high caffeine and theophylline content.
Soothing: White tea's soothing properties help calm irritation and redness, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and let two teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers steep for five minutes to make a chamomile tea concentrate.
Steep one teaspoon of dried cornflower petals in a separate cup of hot water for three minutes.
Combine the chamomile tea concentrate and cornflower tea in a bowl and let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the herbal tea mixture and place them on closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the cotton pads and gently rinse the area with cool water before patting it dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the linden tree, which contain flavonoids and volatile oils that are beneficial for the skin. When applied as a compress or drank as a tea, it can help improve circulation and decrease puffiness around the eyes. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea is known for its calming and soothing effects, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, contributing to overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the sensitive skin around the eyes. This is important for puffy eyes as it helps alleviate inflammation and irritation, providing relief and promoting better circulation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils such as farnesol and linalool.

Diuretic: Littleleaf linden tea acts as a natural diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and puffiness in the eye area. This is essential for puffy eyes as it aids in eliminating excess fluid and toxins, promoting a smoother and more refreshed appearance. The diuretic effect can be attributed to the presence of tannins in the tea.

Antioxidant: Littleleaf linden tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of puffiness. This is important for puffy eyes as antioxidants help maintain the skin's elasticity and firmness, reducing the likelihood of developing bags under the eyes. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the tea.

Calming: Littleleaf linden tea has calming and soothing properties that help relax the eye area, reducing tension and stress-related puffiness. This is significant for puffy eyes as it promotes relaxation and helps alleviate symptoms caused by fatigue or environmental factors. The calming effect can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils like farnesol and linalool.

Astringent: Littleleaf linden tea has mild astringent properties that help tone and tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. This is beneficial for puffy eyes as it helps improve skin texture and reduces the visibility of swollen areas. The astringent properties are primarily due to the tannins present in the tea.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let 1 teaspoon of dried littleleaf linden herb steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the tea and place them over closed eyes for 15 minutes.
Discard the used cotton pads and store the remaining tea in the refrigerator for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contains flavonoids that strengthen blood vessel walls and reduce fluid retention. Drinking pot marigold tea can help decrease inflammation from the inside out, while using the cooled tea bags as a compress on the eyes can further reduce puffiness and soothe tired-looking eyes. The astringent properties of pot marigold tea may also help tighten the skin around the eyes, making them look more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness in eyes by limiting fluid buildup and constricting blood vessels. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamins A and C, helps protect and repair damaged skin cells around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.

Astringent: Pot marigold tea has a mild astringent effect that can tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness and providing a more youthful appearance. This is attributed to the tannins present in the tea.

Diuretic: The diuretic properties of pot marigold tea help eliminate excess fluid from the body, reducing fluid retention and puffiness in the eye area. This is due to the flavonoids and volatile oils found in the plant.

Soothing: Pot marigold tea has soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and redness, providing relief for tired and puffy eyes. This is primarily due to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of the tea.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried marigold flowers and 1 cup of fresh water.
Boil water in a pot, then add marigold flowers and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the marigold flowers from the tea, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the marigold tea and place them on closed eyes for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate irritation. This herbal tea is made from the Euphrasia officinalis plant, which has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various eye ailments. The tannins present in eyebright tea can help to tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, eyebright tea contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can help to soothe tired and puffy eyes, leaving you looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes due to its tannins and flavonoids content.
Astringent: Its astringent properties, caused by tannins, help tighten the skin around the eyes and reduce puffiness.
Antihistamine: Eyebright tea may help alleviate allergic reactions that cause puffy eyes, thanks to its antihistamine properties.
Antimicrobial: The antibacterial and antiviral properties of eyebright tea, attributed to its phenolic acids and essential oils, can help prevent eye infections leading to puffy eyes.
Diuretic: Eyebright tea promotes fluid elimination, reducing water retention and minimizing puffiness around the eyes due to its diuretic effect.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried eyebright herb with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles, resulting in a clear, amber-colored liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature that won't burn or irritate your skin.
Soak two cotton pads in the cooled eyebright tea, then place them over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The tea's natural compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol, can improve blood circulation and constrict blood vessels, which can help alleviate fluid buildup around the eyes. Additionally, lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that can protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, helping to keep the area looking fresh and rejuvenated. Drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a compress can provide a soothing and calming effect, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can also help reduce puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation, making it effective in reducing puffiness around the eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm tea, due to its high content of phenolic acids and flavonoids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage.
Diuretic: Lemon balm tea promotes diuresis, aiding in the elimination of excess fluids that can cause puffiness in the eye area.
Nervine: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and tension, which can help alleviate puffy eyes caused by poor sleep or emotional distress.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm tea, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content, can help prevent eye infections that may contribute to puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, gently rinsing them to remove any dirt or debris.
Crush the leaves in a mortar and pestle to release their oils and aroma.
Place the crushed leaves in a tea infuser or loose leaf tea bag, then steep in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag, allowing the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature for your skin.
Soak two cotton pads in the cooled lemon balm tea, then gently place them over your puffy eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and inflammation. The herb contains compounds called flavonoids, which are known to strengthen blood vessels and capillaries, thereby improving circulation and alleviating fluid buildup in the eye area. Additionally, thyme tea contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of puffiness. Drinking thyme tea or using it as a compress can provide relief and soothe irritated eyes, making it an excellent natural remedy for puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid buildup. This is mainly due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains powerful antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes. Key antioxidants include thymol, lutein, and zeaxanthin.

Diuretic: Thyme tea has diuretic properties that promote the elimination of excess fluid from the body, which can help alleviate puffiness in the eyes. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils like carvacrol and thymol.

Astringent: Thyme tea's astringent properties help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. The tannins in thyme tea are responsible for its astringent effects.

Calming: Thyme tea has calming and soothing properties that can help reduce stress and relax the eye area, which may help prevent or alleviate puffy eyes. This is due to the presence of various compounds like camphene and myrcene, which have a relaxing effect on the body.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes until it's warm, but not hot.
Crush 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves and add them to the warm water.
Steep the thyme in the water for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves and let it cool down completely.
Soak two cotton pads in the thyme tea and place them on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce fluid buildup in the eye area. Additionally, marjoram tea contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Drinking marjoram tea or using compresses soaked in the tea can provide relief and improve the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup, thanks to its active compounds like terpinene-4-ol and γ-terpinene.
Diuretic: Marjoram tea promotes fluid elimination, which helps alleviate puffy eyes caused by fluid retention, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in marjoram tea protects the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles, thanks to active compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.
Calming: Marjoram tea has a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and redness that can contribute to puffy eyes, due to its active constituents like linalool and α-terpineol.

Circulation-boosting: Marjoram tea promotes blood circulation, helping to decrease puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by improving oxygen and nutrient supply to the skin, thanks to its active compounds like 1,8-cineole and camphor.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes until it reaches a warm temperature.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried marjoram leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh marjoram leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the warm water over the marjoram leaves and cover the teapot or infuser.
Let the marjoram leaves steep in the water for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the marjoram tea into a cup and allow it to cool down before using it as a compress on puffy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tea helps with puffy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The tea is made from the petals and hips of the dog rose plant, which are rich in vitamin C and antioxidants that can help to strengthen the skin around the eyes and improve circulation. This can help to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes, dark circles, and fine lines. Additionally, the cool temperature of the tea can help to soothe and calm the skin, providing further relief from puffiness and inflammation. Drinking dog rose tea can also help to improve overall skin health and reduce inflammation throughout the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup. This is due to the presence of tannins, flavonoids, and vitamin C.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in dog rose tea helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to puffiness and aging. The active constituents responsible for this are flavonoids, vitamin C, and carotenoids.
Astringent: Dog rose tea acts as an astringent, tightening and toning the skin around the eyes, which can help reduce the appearance of puffy eyes. This is due to the presence of tannins.

Diuretic: The diuretic properties of dog rose tea help eliminate excess fluid from the body, including the area around the eyes, thereby reducing puffiness. This is attributed to the presence of pectin and vitamin C.

Soothing: Dog rose tea has soothing properties that help alleviate irritation and inflammation, providing relief for puffy eyes. This is due to the presence of essential fatty acids, flavonoids, and vitamin C.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried dog rose petals with 1 cup of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea into a clean container, discarding the used petals.
Soak two cotton pads in the dog rose tea and place them over closed eyes for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with puffy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation. The tannins in witch hazel act as a natural astringent, helping to tighten the skin around the eyes and decrease the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, witch hazel contains antioxidants that can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking witch hazel tea can also help reduce inflammation in the body, leading to a reduction in puffy eyes. Overall, the soothing and anti-inflammatory effects of witch hazel make it an ideal natural remedy for puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea can reduce inflammation and swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes, providing relief for puffy eyes. This property is due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.

Astringent: Witch hazel tea helps tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles. This is attributed to the high tannin content, which constricts blood vessels and reduces fluid accumulation.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage and promote cell regeneration, aiding in the reduction of puffy eyes. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids, which neutralize free radicals and support collagen production.

Soothing: Witch hazel tea has a calming effect on the skin, alleviating irritation and redness associated with puffy eyes. This soothing property is attributed to the presence of gallic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant effects.

Venotonic: Witch hazel tea can help strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation, reducing the likelihood of fluid accumulation and puffiness around the eyes. This is due to the presence of rutin, a bioflavonoid that supports venous health and reduces capillary permeability.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes, so it's warm to the touch.
In a teapot or heat-resistant container, add 1 tablespoon of dried witch hazel leaves.
Pour the warm water over the leaves, cover, and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean container and let it cool down completely in the refrigerator.
Soak two cotton pads in the chilled tea and place them over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with puffy eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness. The tea's soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate stress and tension, which can contribute to eye puffiness. Additionally, lavender tea contains antioxidants that can improve blood circulation and reduce fluid buildup in the eye area. To use lavender tea for puffy eyes, simply soak a couple of tea bags in hot water, let them cool, and then place them over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. This can help reduce puffiness, soothe tired eyes, and leave you feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in puffy eyes by limiting the production of inflammatory chemicals like prostaglandins (active constituents
Diuretic: It promotes fluid elimination, helping to alleviate puffiness and fluid retention around the eyes (active constituents
Analgesic: English lavender tea has pain-relieving properties that can soothe and calm irritated eyes (active constituent
Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, promoting a healthier and younger-looking appearance (active constituents

Antimicrobial: English lavender tea's antimicrobial properties can help prevent eye infections and promote overall eye health, reducing the risk of puffy eyes caused by bacterial or viral infections (active constituents
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the lavender tea to remove the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the lavender tea.
Place the soaked cotton pads on closed puffy eyes for 10-15 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. First, steep two tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Once cooled, place the tea bags over your closed eyes for about 15-20 minutes. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas help reduce swelling and soothe the skin around your eyes. For best results, do this daily or whenever needed.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal teas include eye inflammation, dark circles, and eye strain. Chamomile tea, when cooled, can reduce puffiness and soothe irritation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce the appearance of dark circles and improve blood circulation. For eye strain, cucumber or chamomile tea compresses can alleviate discomfort and relax the eyes. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional for persistent or severe symptoms.
